q: In which year did Christiaan Huygens introduce the wave theory of light?
Every point on a wavefront may be considered as a source of secondary wavelets.
The new position of a wavefront is the ___ of the wavelets.
Secondary wavelets spread out in all directions with the speed ___ .
Secondary wavelets will have the same ___ and speed as the propagating wave.
Huygens' principle explained how the light propagates from a light source.
Christiaan Huygens was a ___ physicist.
What is the envelope of secondary wavelets called?
The secondary wavelets have a different frequency than the propagating wave.
Wave optics includes the ___ characteristics of light.
A surface associated with a propagating wave which is a locus of all points in the wave that have the same phase is a ___.
On a particular wavefront, at a given moment of time, all particles of the medium are undergoing the same phase.
Wavefront is always ___ to the direction of propagation (or) rays.
How many types of wavefront are there?
A plane wavefront transforms into a spherical wavefront when it is far away from the source.
The angular position of a point of a periodic wave is known as the ___.
The different states of the periodic waves at the same instant is represented by ___ difference.
If the phase difference is $2\pi, 4\pi, 6\pi, \dots$, these two positions are in ___.
If phase difference is $\pi, 3\pi, 5\pi, \dots$, the positions are completely out of phase.
The distance between two points measured along the direction of propagation of the wave is the ___ difference.
If path difference is $0, \lambda, 2\lambda, 3\lambda, \dots$, it is said the waves are in ___.
If path difference is $\frac{\lambda}{2}, \frac{3\lambda}{2}, \frac{5\lambda}{2}, \dots$, the waves are completely out of phase.
The relation between phase difference and path difference is $\Delta\phi = $ ___.
Young was able to explain his experiment result as an ___ phenomenon.
When a plane wave passes through a slit, what type of wavelet emerges?
Waves emitted from two slits in Young's experiment have different frequencies.
Two waves are coherent if they have the same frequency, same amplitude and are in ___.
The interference pattern consists of a series of bright and dark parallel lines called ___ .
Constructive interference occurs at the centre of the screen because the waves travel the ___ distance.
The bright fringe at the centre of the screen is called the central bright line.
If path difference is an integral number of wavelengths ($0, \lambda, 2\lambda, \dots$), the waves interfere ___.
Constructive interference results in ___ fringes.
If path difference is a half-integral number of wavelengths, the waves interfere ___.
Destructive interference results in a dark fringe.
The position of bright and dark fringes on the screen is denoted by ___ .
For a bright fringe, the path difference $d \sin \theta$ is equal to ___.
In the formula for bright fringes, $m$ is called the order of bright fringe.
The zeroth order bright fringe corresponds to the central bright line at $\theta = $ ___.
For dark fringes, the path difference $d \sin \theta$ is equal to ___ .
The distance between two adjacent bright (or dark) fringes is called the ___.
Fringe width $\Delta y$ is expressed as $\frac{\lambda D}{d}$.
The position of fringes and fringe width depend on the ___ of the light source.
Bright and dark fringes are formed alternatively where the bright fringe has ___ intensity.
The intensity of the dark fringe is zero.
Monochromatic light means that it has a ___ wavelength.
The bending of waves around the corners of an obstacle is defined as ___.
Diffraction also occurs when waves pass through an aperture into the region of geometrical shadow.
The amount of bending depends on the wavelength of light relative to the ___ of the aperture.
If the aperture is much larger than the wavelength of light, the bending will be ___.
Light waves diffract around objects and create bright and dark fringes.
Which object's sharp edges can be used to demonstrate light diffraction?
The two main classes of diffraction are Fresnel diffraction and ___ diffraction.
In Fresnel diffraction, the source and screen are at a ___ distance from the obstacle.
In Fraunhofer diffraction, there is an infinite distance between the source and the screen.
A single-slit diffraction pattern consists of a bright central portion called the ___.
Successive bright fringes in a single-slit pattern become ___ intense proceeding outward.
Bright and dark fringes in diffraction are created by interference of diffracted light waves.
For the first minimum in single-slit diffraction, the path difference $a \sin \theta$ is equal to ___.
Generally for the $m^{th}$ minimum in single-slit diffraction, $a \sin \theta = $ ___ .
The width of the central maximum in single-slit diffraction is $2 \times$ the ___ .
The width of the central maximum depends on the width of the slit.
A narrower slit causes a ___ central maximum.
The formula for the width of the central maximum is ___.
In diffraction by a single slit, $a$ represents the slit width.
At the central maximum, secondary wavelets reach the point $P_0$ in the ___.
An optical device consisting of a large number of parallel slits of same width separated by equal opaque spaces is a ___.
A diffraction grating can disperse light into its wavelength components.
Diffraction grating is used for the purpose of ___ light into spectra.
The number of lines (or slits) per unit length is called the ___.
Two types of grating are transmission grating and reflection grating.
What material can be carved with a sharp tool to manufacture a diffraction grating?
The condition for maximum intensity in a diffraction grating is $d \sin \theta = $ ___ .
In the grating equation, $d$ is the distance between ___.
The value of $d$ is equal to $1/N$, where $N$ is the number of slits per unit length.
At the centre of the white light spectrum ($m=0$), there is no ___ .
In the first order diffraction spectrum, which light diffracts at a greater angle?
Longer wavelength light diffracts at a greater angle than shorter wavelength light.
A compact disc is an example of ___ gratings.
The tracks of a compact disc act as a diffraction grating, producing a separation of ___.
Diffraction gratings are used as dispersive elements in spectrographic instruments.
Diffraction grating is a crucial tool in the study of atomic and ___ compositions.
Modern applications use gratings with several ___ slits per centimetre.
The condition for maximum intensity for a grating is the same as that for a Young’s double slit.
In the first order spectrum, red light has a ___ wavelength than violet light.
What type of surface does a compact disc have?
The angular position of a periodic wave is known as the ___.
If waves emitted from two sources have the same frequency and constant phase difference, the sources are coherent.
The modification in the distribution of light energy when two or more waves superimpose is ___.
The condition for constructive interference is that the path difference should be equal to an ___ multiple of the wavelength.
The condition for destructive interference is that the path difference should be equal to an odd integral multiple of half wavelength.
Monochromatic light means that it has a ___.
Diffraction of light is defined as the ___ of waves around the corners of an obstacle.
A diffraction grating consists of a large number of parallel slits of same width separated by equal opaque spaces.
The position of $m^{th}$ minimum $y$ is the distance from the ___.
A wavefront is perpendicular to the direction of ___ .
